{"key_event":"Liberal Empire reforms","reasoning":"Civil society recovered markedly as amnesty, parliamentary debate, opposition electoral gains, strike legalization, the International, and looser press/public-meeting laws rebuilt organized opposition from the repressive 1850s. State capacity remained very strong through bureaucracy, railways, policing, Haussmannization, and territorial consolidation, but its unchecked dominance and prestige declined with liberal concessions and foreign-policy failures.","society_change":1.2,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":8.4}
